item.title: 5371a-2025-26: International & Domestic Removals & Storage Services
item.description: International & Domestic Removal & Storage Services

item.title: Proc-9177441: Phosphine technical coordinator.
item.description:
GRDC is seeking a suitably qualified technical coordinator/consultant to provide project management support to the portfolio of phosphine investments. 

 It is anticipated that this role reflects 1.5-2 days per week (on average) over 2 years for a suitably experienced consultant.

item.title: S-EST11298: Royal Military College Building Refurbishment, ACT
item.description: Royal Military College Building Refurbishment

item.title: AZ6622: ESTS4000PH6 Facilities to support DDG Combat System Enhancement- Notice of potential upcoming procurement
item.description:
This is a notice for a potential upcoming procurement for the Head Contractor package of works (Defence Head Contract HC-GMP) under project ESTS4000PH6 Facilities to Support the Hobart Class Destroyer Combat System Enhancement Capability Project (the Project). 

SEA4000PH6 aims to deliver an Aegis Baseline 9 and Australian Interface combat system enhancement to the Hobart Class Guided Missile Destroyers. The facilities component of the Project focuses on delivering suitable land-based facilities to support SEA4000PH6 prior to Q3 2029. 

Potential tenderers are advised that the procurement strategy for the Project involves the engagement of a HC-GMP to deliver the Works via a 2-stage Invitation to Register (ITR) and Request for Tender (RFT).

item.description: Graphic and digital marketing services, as well as event management support for virtual events.

item.description: The AFP has an ongoing requirement to procure training and operational munitions.

item.title: 10032193: Norfolk Island Kingston Pier Dredging and Pier Remediation Project
item.description:
The Department is seeking to engage a supplier to deepen and widen the 

channel seabed adjacent to Kingston Pier at Norfolk Island, install a navigation 

aid, and to undertake repairs and strengthening to Pier.

item.title: MGF_17: Supply of Medical Grade Footwear to eligible persons
item.description:
NOTE: existing suppliers need not reapply

The Medical Grade Footwear (MGF) Programme is an important part of DVA’s health care arrangements and is part of the Rehabilitation Appliance Programme (RAP).  The MGF Programme provides a nation-wide access to a wide range of MGF and related services for entitled persons.

item.title: CNS16: Department of Veterans' Affairs Community Nursing Services Invitation
item.description: Department of Veterans' Affairs invites interested suppliers of Community Nursing Services to apply to provide these services from 1 July 2016.

item.title: 10031971: TSSI - Trains, Systems and Systems Integration
item.description: The Authority seeks to engage suitably experienced Early Contractor Involvement (ECI) Partners on a fee-for-service, collaborative basis to develop the designs and program for the project, with an initial cooperative phase (Project Conformance and Validation Period), followed by a competitive ECI process finishing with a Proposal Submission by ECI Partners.

item.title: OPC- 2026-02: Bills Printing and Distribution
item.description: The successful Tenderer will be required to print and distribute Bills to the Senate or the House of Representatives, and to other agencies as required by OPC.

item.title: DCET2026-01A: EST10003 RAAF Base Tindal Renewable Energy Microgrid – Design Services
item.description:
The Defence Renewable Energy and Energy Security Program (DREESP) is looking to design a new microgrid renewable energy system for a Defence Base in the Northern Territory. As part of this process DREESP are seeking a Design Services Consultant to deliver the system design. 

 

Deliverables include design documentation for all design stages encompassing:  

o System layout and configuration  

o Renewable energy resource assessment and integration strategy  

o Energy storage system sizing and specification  

o Control system architecture and communication protocols  

o Cost estimation and feasibility considerations  

o Clear technical basis for procurement and construction planning.

item.title: AFMA25-26-3: Provision of Fishery Monitoring services for the Southern Bluefin Tuna (SBT) Fishery
item.description: This Approach to Market (ATM) as a Request for Tender (RFT) is for the provision of Monitoring Services for the farming sector of the SBT Fishery that operates at sea and around Port Lincoln SA.

item.title: Antarctic ATM26/95: ATM 26/95 SOra Main Works Package
item.description: Construction services relating to the Southern Ocean Research Aquarium (SOra) main works package.

item.title: Health/E25-657123: Development, Delivery and Evaluation of Medicare Education Resources for University Medical Students
item.description: The Department of Health, Disability and Ageing is seeking to engage university medical schools in a pilot project to develop Medicare education resources, deliver the resources to their students, and evaluate the effectiveness of the resources and delivery methods.

item.title: AIMSRFT785: AIMS NT Small Research Vessel Replacement
item.description: Replacement Vessel for RV Capricornus - The Australian Institute of Marine Science (AIMS) requires the design, construction, supply, and delivery of a new fit for purpose small research vessel (the “Vessel”) to be delivered to AIMS Darwin, Northern Territory.

COMMONWEALTH LEASE

PRESTON AND SURROUNDS VIC

item.title: NRD07664: Charles Perkins House Lighting Upgrade
item.description:
The lighting system at Charles Perkins House (CPH) is at end of life, unserviceable, and no longer compliant with current safety and performance standards.    

The existing lighting system is Clipsal Schneider DALI, as per the specifications in the Approach to Market document.    

The full LED upgrade must:

• Ensure compliance with workplace safety and illumination standards.

• Support the NIAA’s Net Zero targets through energy-efficient infrastructure.

• Achieve recommended lux levels across all workstations.

item.title: PPD/DA/71746/1: Boeing Business Jets
item.description: Boeing Business Jets

item.title: PROC-9177419: Building out the Nitrogen Mission
item.description:
Australian grain growers are among the most innovative in the world but need a breakthrough in nitrogen (N) management. GRDC has been investing in new frontiers in nitrogen management for many years and is well positioned to scale up that investment in a structured, long-term, and strategic manner via the building out of the Nitrogen Mission. The recent fertiliser supply crisis exemplifies the need to do so. Specifically, GRDC has ambitions to decrease long-term (historical) N costs as a percentage of crop revenue by 50% or more, halve current N losses to the environment, and decrease emissions intensity associated with N usage by at least 20%. In the process GRDC aims to build out world-leading research capacity and science and technology innovation in N management in the Australian grains industry. The opportunity for partners is to coinvest with GRDC in delivering a breakthrough that unlocks major economic, social, and environmental benefits in the Australian grains industry, and potentially, other agricultural markets internationally. Even a 15% reduction in long-term nitrogen fertiliser costs while maintaining yields is estimated to deliver over $1 billion AUD of value to Australian grain growers through improved gross margins and reduced financial risk.  

As a partner, GRDC is one of the world’s leading grain research organisations. GRDC has a large existing research and development (R&D) portfolio in N and related areas, extensive research, development, and innovation networks in Australia and internationally, a strong balance sheet, and a long-term outlook. Australia is also an ideal market to incubate, develop, and scale world-leading technologies in N management given the culture of innovation, technology adoption, and adaptability in the Australian grains industry. The information below describes the opportunity for partners in more detail and the process involved.

item.title: JSD/RFT/76225/1: LAND 4140 Tactical Communications Network (TCN) Tactical Edge Radio (TER)

item.title: DPC-2017: Invitation to provide Day Procedure Centre Services to the Veteran Community
item.description: The Department of Veterans’ Affairs (DVA) is seeking to contract with eligible stand-alone private day hospitals (referred to as Day Procedure Centres or DPCs) to provide DPC services for DVA Entitled Persons across Australia from 1 October 2017 onwards.

item.title: PHS-16: Private Hospital Services
item.description: DVA is seeking the provision of Private Hospital services for Entitled Persons by publishing this Invitation to Provide Private Hospital Services to the Veteran Community.
